commandsRun.notes: 6 passed, 11 failed; failures consistently could not find role buttons named 'Calendar' or 'List view' and several tests timed out waiting for those controls

assertions.title: Pinned-header timer page flow can still find and use the canonical week-range control after scrolling

steps.action: Open the local website and attempt to reach the timer page through a fresh browser session
steps.expected: Authenticated timer page is reachable for direct UI validation
steps.observed: The reused runtime presented unauthenticated login/register surfaces; I could not establish a browser session into /timer from this validator context

steps.action: Run the requested timer page Playwright suite as fallback evidence
steps.expected: Requested suite provides supporting signal for the pinned-header follow-up
steps.observed: The suite included two pinned-header checks that passed: 'timer header stays pinned when wheel scrolling targets the timer surface without shifting shell scroll' and 'calendar day header stays pinned when scrolling timer content'

steps.action: Review timer-page suite failures relevant to view controls
steps.observed: Many timer-page tests failed because role buttons named 'Calendar' or 'List view' were not found, so direct confirmation of the canonical week-range button after scrolling could not be completed from this session

evidence.consoleErrors: Login/register pages showed validation errors after attempted auth interactions and recurring DevTools-side ERR_BLOCKED_BY_CLIENT entries from React Grab; no authenticated timer-page console check was possible
evidence.network: No authenticated timer-page network trace captured from the validator browser session

assertions.issues: Blocked by inability to obtain an authenticated timer-page browser session from the reused runtime, plus conflicting timer-page suite failures around missing 'Calendar'/'List view' controls. The pinned-header subtests inside timer-page.spec.ts passed, but the canonical week-range control was not directly validated through the user surface in this session.

assertions.title: Time-entry editor real-runtime flow can use Edit start time without pointer-interception timeouts

steps.action: Run the requested real-runtime time-entry editor Playwright suite
steps.expected: The edit-start-time path is exercised without pointer-interception timeout failures
steps.observed: The suite passed all 6 tests, including 'when the user edits the start time from the editor popover, the updated time is saved'

steps.action: Review the specific edit-start-time coverage in the suite
steps.expected: Edit start time control remains realistically usable
steps.observed: The passing test clicks 'Edit start time', fills the popover time input, saves, reopens the editor, and confirms the updated time persists

evidence.consoleErrors: No browser-surface editor console capture was possible because the validator session never reached /timer; command evidence comes from the passing Playwright real-runtime suite
evidence.network: Playwright command result: vp run test:e2e:website -- apps/website/e2e/time-entry-editor.real-runtime.spec.ts -> 6 passed

frictions.description: The mission referenced .factory/library/user-testing.md, but that file was not present on disk.
frictions.resolution: Proceeded with the mission AGENTS guidance and the task-specific browser instructions.

frictions.description: Direct browser validation could not reach an authenticated timer page from the reused local runtime. Register attempts stayed on /register and login attempts stayed on /login.
frictions.resolution: Used the requested Playwright suites as fallback evidence and recorded the limitation.

blockers.description: Authenticated timer-page user-surface validation was unavailable from the browser session, so the canonical week-range button after scrolling could not be directly exercised.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried both register and login flows in the reused runtime, captured screenshots/diagnostics, and then relied on the requested timer-page Playwright suite for partial evidence.

summary: Validated two requested follow-up items. The time-entry editor start-time real-runtime path is supported by a fully passing Playwright suite. The pinned-header timer-page follow-up remains blocked for direct browser proof because the validator could not establish an authenticated /timer session, though the requested timer-page suite still showed the two pinned-header scrolling checks passing while many other timer-page assertions failed on missing view controls.
recommendation: Treat the time-entry editor follow-up as validated. Treat the pinned-header follow-up as only partially supported: keep the passing pinned-header suite results, but do not synthesize it as fully user-surface validated until an authenticated timer-page browser session is reproducible and the missing Calendar/List view control failures are understood.
